directive.title: Basic application shell with navigation structure

auditor.summary: The generated code implements the core application shell with AppShell, Header, Sidebar, routing, and loading states as specified. The architecture follows Next.js 14 App Router patterns with proper code-splitting, Suspense boundaries, and accessibility features. Key issues: (1) HIGH - LucideIcon type imported from barrel violates constraint, (2) MEDIUM - Header missing 'use client' directive will cause hydration errors, (3) MEDIUM - Navigation audit trail 'from' field uses unreliable document.referrer, (4) LOW - Skeleton card count mismatch, header layout issue with collapsed sidebar. The build is recoverable with fixes. Score 78/100 reflects these issues against the 20 acceptance criteria.

top_issues.description: Imports LucideIcon type from 'lucide-react' barrel export. Constraint explicitly prohibits barrel imports from lucide-react. This violates acceptance criterion #20 and the constraint 'lucide-react imports must be individual named imports (not barrel) to enable tree-shaking'.

Navigation event 'from' field uses document.referrer which is unreliable for client-side navigation. It will be empty or show previous full URL, not the previous route within the app. This makes the audit trail incomplete.
Header component is not a client component ('use client' directive missing) but uses interactive elements (buttons with onClick). This will cause a hydration error in Next.js 14 App Router.
